title: Analytical Methodology — eleven discipline pages from a Georgetown Applied Intelligence curriculum, treating each analytical method as its own working surface
public_claim: A Georgetown Applied Intelligence methodology pillar that treats eleven analytical disciplines as distinct working surfaces.
distinctive_mechanism: It differentiates all-source, counterintelligence, crisis management, counter-operations, cyber, defense/diplomacy/intelligence, emerging-threats, foresight, homeland-security, intelligence-cycle, and public-service analysis by case anchor, source demand, method fit, output form, and cross-method composition. Meridian Network, Operation GRAY DAWN, the Caracas Question, and HS-style signal-detection moves function as worked anchors rather than empty labels.
use_value: It matters because experts need to see which method belongs to which kind of problem, when one discipline can borrow from another, and how eleven adjacent pages become a working method library rather than a list of course topics.
